problem.function: State that finite-resolution HgCdTe maps are routinely interpreted as material-uniformity fields even though their apparent variance depends on the measurement kernel, calibration, field of view, and spatial dependence.

method.function: Describe an integrated analytical framework for kernel-filtered covariance, scale recoverability, common calibration uncertainty, covariance-family closure, finite-map moments, and same-raster cross-scale covariance.

analytical_result.function: State the minimum decision-changing results: one scale cannot identify variance and correlation scale; two Gaussian-benchmark scales estimate but cannot test the family; a third scale supplies closure; common scale calibration sets an absolute-length floor; correlated finite maps and shared scales contain less information than nominal counts imply.

real_map_demonstration.function: State that a public 24 by 24 CdSeTe Gaussian-fitted PL peak-wavelength map is used only as a cross-material method demonstration; added numerical smoothing retains 11.24 percent of unsmoothed variance at four pixels and exposes strong same-raster and finite-field sensitivity.
required_terms: numerical scales from one raster are dependent

limitation.function: State explicitly that no qualifying public source-native HgCdTe multiresolution package was found, the CdSeTe native sample-plane kernel is unmeasured, and the manuscript reports no HgCdTe specimen covariance family or physical correlation length.
required_terms: no physical correlation length is reported

practical_consequence.function: Conclude that credible spatial-uniformity studies must report calibrated kernels, at least three informative scales for family closure, finite-map covariance, cross-scale dependence, and predeclared field and edge conventions.
